name: Indus Phase-13 - validity tests + alternative metrics + Sanskrit adversarial + PMI baseline
description: Phase-13 successor to Phase-12. Wires every Phase-13 deliverable into one JSON-defined graph. (B) Length stratification with KFoldRunner per stratum for Tamil + Luwian + Greek so we can read recall on medium/long inscriptions, not just the trivial-match short ones. (A.1) Three negative-control branches per main language with CorpusPermuter -> SA -> recall, fed into a DeltaRecall node that emits real - permuted as the headline-quality metric. (A.2) MultiWordHitRate node per main language, restricted to inscriptions of decoded length >= 3, again the same-headline view but on the cases that matter. (G) RoleShuffledNullDistribution on Tamil: shuffles role_table assignments across signs, runs SA, and reports z-score of observed real-mode recall vs. that null. If the role classifier is decorative, observed recall sits inside the null. (C) LogogramAwareValueRoleMap promotes the top-30 Tamil DEDR / Tamil-Brahmi attested words to role 'logogram'; a parallel sa_tamil_logo branch uses the new role map so the 23 Phase-12 logogram signs have legitimate logogram-tagged values to land on. (D) SignMappingConsistency consumes proposed_mapping from Tamil / Luwian / Greek / Sanskrit / PMIBigramAligner and reports pairwise Jaccard + per-sign value-agreement. (E) Sanskrit adversarial branches: Sanskrit LM + Sanskrit vocab full SA + recall (E1); Tamil LM + Sanskrit vocab cross-vocab control (E2); Sanskrit LM + Tamil vocab cross-vocab control (E3). All three feed a DeltaRecall vs. CorpusPermuter. (F) PMIBigramAligner runs the Berg-Kirkpatrick / Cotterell-Eisner alignment baseline on Tamil and Greek bigrams, no SA, and compares its proposed_mapping to SA mappings via SignMappingConsistency. Result is written to reports/indus_phase13_full.json.

data.label: P13-C: SA Tamil w/ logogram VRM
P13-E2: Tamil-LM SA (used w/ Sanskrit vocab)
P13-E2: Tamil-LM mapping vs Sanskrit vocab
P13-E3: Sanskrit-LM mapping vs Tamil vocab
